member.name: James MacCleary
member.synopsis: James MacCleary is the Liberal Democrat MP for Lewes , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Defence).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will provide an update on the plan to implement a new offsets regime to support UK defence companies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Defence Industrial Strategy (DIS), the Ministry of Defence is currently exploring an offset policy to back British businesses and help deliver the DIS vision to make defence an engine for growth.

writtenQuestions.heading: Salisbury Plain
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department is taking to assist the Diocese of Salisbury and the Churches Conservation Trust in the preservation of St Giles Church Imber.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Churches Conservation Trust is responsible for the preservation of St Giles Church in Imber. The Ministry of Defence (MOD) supports the Trust by cutting the grass, sweeping the road and providing rubbish collection facilities during the open days.

writtenQuestions.heading: Salisbury Plain
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department is taking to maintain the historic buildings in Imber village and the wider Salisbury Plain Training Area.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no historic listed buildings in Imber village other than St Giles Church, which is not owned or maintained by the Ministry of Defence. Across Salisbury Plain Training Area (SPTA), there are 14 historic listed buildings, managed by Landmarc Suppo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Boxer Vehicles: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many Boxer vehicles are scheduled to be delivered to the Army in (a) 2026, (b) 2027, (c) 2028, (d) 2029, (e) 2030, (f) 2031 and (g) 2032.
writtenQuestions.answerText: 100 of the 623 contracted Boxer vehicles have been delivered. The remaining profile will be continuously delivered over the next seven years; however, it is not possible to release the detail requested as it is commercially sensitive.

writtenQuestions.heading: Armoured Fighting Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many Jackal vehicles are in service in (a) the British Army (b) the Royal Air Force Regiment (c) the Royal Marines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are 639 Jackal vehicles in service in the British Army and 61 Jackal vehicles in service in the Royal Marines. I am unable to disclose the number of Jackal vehicles in service with the Royal Air Force Regiment as due to operational security, we do n...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armoured Fighting Vehicles: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many Jackal 3(E) have been delivered.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are 639 Jackal vehicles in service in the British Army and 61 Jackal vehicles in service in the Royal Marines. I am unable to disclose the number of Jackal vehicles in service with the Royal Air Force Regiment as due to operational security, we do n...

writtenQuestions.heading: Rifles: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what progress has been made on Project Cairns.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to my answer to Question 72366, which remains extant: The L7A2 General Purpose machine Gun (GPMG) is expected to be replaced under Project CAIRNS. Project CAIRNS is currently a pre-concept project and as such formal consideration o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Rifles: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, when does he estimate Project Grayburn will complete the concept phase and enter the assessment phase.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to my answer to Question 125983, which remains extant: Project GRAYBURN is currently in the concept phase with the aim of identifying and understanding requirements. As the exact delivery schedule will be determined in due course, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Defence: Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether his Department owned the Mantell building in the University of Sussex campus.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is no record of the Mantell building at the University of Sussex being purchased or owned by the Department.

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Defence: Palantir
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the Answer of 4 June 2026 to Question 4946 on Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: Palantir, whether the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office has access to services provided under Min
writtenQuestions.answerText: No other Governmental departments have access to the Palantir services under the Ministry of Defence Contracts and there are no provisions for other Government departments to have access.

writtenQuestions.heading: Firearms: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what progress his Department has made on Project Troubler.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I provided to the hon. Member for Huntingdon (Ben Obese-Jecty) on 4 February 2026 to Question 110962: Project TROUBLER is currently in the concept phase. As part of this phase, performance, interoperability and comple...

writtenQuestions.heading: Rifles: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what progress his Department has made on Project Shamer.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I provided to the hon. Member for Huntingdon (Ben Obese-Jecty) on 5 November 2025 to Question 85997: Project Shamer is a Defence procurement project. For reasons of operational security, I cannot provide any further d...

writtenQuestions.heading: Unmanned Air Systems
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether delivery of an Air-Air Refuelling mission capability remains within the scope of Project VANQUISH.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Project VANQUISH will test the feasibility of launching and recovering an uncrewed Collaborative Combat Aircraft from a Queen Elizabeth Class carrier. Evidence from Project VANQUISH will inform future capability decisions, including air-to-air refuelling ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Salisbury Plain
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many visitors to Imber village used the Imber Bus each year over the last 5 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not hold records of the number of visitors to Imber Village or how many of these visitors use the Imber bus.

writtenQuestions.heading: Salisbury Plain
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many visitors has Imber village had each year over the last 5 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not hold records of the number of visitors to Imber Village or how many of these visitors use the Imber bus.

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Submarines: Decommissioning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the Answer of 8 June 2026 to Question 4943 Nuclear Submarines: Decommissioning, whether pyroprocessing is being used to recycle used fuel from decommissioned nuclear submarines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No. The Ministry of Defence does not recycle spent nuclear fuel from decommissioned submarines.

writtenQuestions.heading: Ukraine: Armoured Fighting Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many Stormer HVMs have been sent to Ukraine.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence has sent six Stormer air defence systems to Ukraine, alongside hundreds of Starstreak High Velocity Missiles (HVM) and Lightweight Multi-Role Missiles, which are the primary weapons utilised by the Stormer.

writtenQuestions.heading: Fire and Rescue Services: Military Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, pursuant to the Answer of 8 June 2026 to Question 5016 on Military Aid: Fire and Rescue Services, what provision is there for a civilian national aerial firefighting capability i
writtenQuestions.answerText: Fire and rescue services are fully aware of the growing risk of wildfires and, working with their Local Resilience Forum partners, are responsible for maintaining the necessary plans and capabilities required to manage that risk. Where aerial support is c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Ajax Vehicles: Noise
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, has the ARGUS engineering vehicle encountered the same vibrational and safety issues as the Ajax vehicles.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The recent Army Safety Investigation, commissioned in response to Exercise Titan Storm, underlined that all variants of Ajax are safe to operate. Specifically, measured vibration levels were below legal safety limits. I will continue to keep the House inf...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ajax Vehicles: Noise
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, has the ARES armoured personnel carrier encountered the same vibrational and safety issues as the Ajax vehicles.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The recent Army Safety Investigation, commissioned in response to Exercise Titan Storm, underlined that all variants of Ajax are safe to operate. Specifically, measured vibration levels were below legal safety limits. I will continue to keep the House inf...
